label: Possessives
description: mio, tuo, suo and the rest: the possessive plus its article, agreeing with the thing owned, and the family-noun exception.

label: Possessive + article, agreeing
description: The possessive takes the definite article and agrees with the thing owned, not the owner.

attributes.canonical_example: il mio libro, la mia casa, i miei amici, le sue sorelle
attributes.common_miss: mio libro (no article); il mio casa (agreement); suo for the owner's gender

label: Family nouns drop the article
description: A singular, unmodified family noun takes no article; the article returns in the plural or when the noun is modified.

attributes.canonical_example: mia madre, mio fratello; ma la mia mamma, i miei fratelli
attributes.common_miss: la mia madre; il mio fratello

label: Possessive pronouns
description: The possessive used on its own (with the article) to mean mine, yours, and so on.

attributes.canonical_example: Questo e il mio; Il tuo dov'e?
attributes.common_miss: dropping the article on the standalone possessive

label: suo: his, her, your (formal)
description: suo agrees with the thing owned, so context resolves whether it is his, her or your (formal).
